---
execute:
  echo: false
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    fig.path: figures/
    fig.pos: H
params:
  uni:
    values: [CU, AU, UQ]
  pri_bio: Your.Name
  project_code: ABC2026-001ABC
  partner: collab_partner
  partner_email: collab_partner@email.com.au
  report_title: Informative title for short report
format:
  aagi-html+report:
    toc: true
    self-contained: true
date: last-modified
date-format: long
bibliography: references.bib
csl: _extensions/AAGI-AUS/aagi/assets/biometrics.csl

title: !expr params$report_title
subtitle: !expr paste("Report for", params$project_code)
author:
  - !expr sprintf("Prepared for: %s (%s)", params$partner, params$project_code)
  - !expr sprintf("Prepared by: %s", params$pri_bio)
email: !expr if (params$uni == "CU") "cbada@curtin.edu.au" else if (params$uni == "AU") paste0(params$pri_bio, "@adelaide.edu.au") else paste0(params$pri_bio, "@uq.edu.au")
---

::: {.aagi-titlepage}

![](_extensions/AAGI-AUS/aagi/assets/AAGI_logo.svg){.aagi-logo}

:::: {.aagi-title-block}
**Analytics for the Australian Grains Industry**  
**`r switch(params$uni,
  CU = "Curtin University (AAGI-CU)",
  AU = "University of Adelaide (AAGI-AU)",
  UQ = "University of Queensland (AAGI-UQ)",
  "University Name (AAGI-XX)")`**  
**AAGI Program Report**

# `r params$report_title`

## Report for `r params$project_code`

Prepared for: **`r params$partner` (`r params$partner_email`)**  
Prepared by: **`r params$pri_bio`**, Name 2  
Correspondence email: <`r if (params$uni == "CU") "cbada@curtin.edu.au"
  else if (params$uni == "AU") paste0(params$pri_bio, "@adelaide.edu.au")
  else paste0(params$pri_bio, "@uq.edu.au")`>  
_Last updated: `r Sys.Date()`_
::::

![](_extensions/AAGI-AUS/aagi/assets/AAGI_SP_Logo_Block-Portrait.svg){.aagi-partners}

:::

# Executive Summary {#executive-summary}

Executive summary goes here.
Summarise the work provided by AAGI and the main results.

# Key findings

-   Finding 1
-   Finding 2
-   Finding 3

::: {.aagi-note}
**NOTE** — This is an example of an AAGI note callout.
:::

# Methods (brief)

Briefly describe the analysis approach and any key assumptions.

# Results (brief)

Summarise the primary results, with 1–2 key figures/tables as needed.

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
